overview.description: Edgewater is a narrow, mile-wide borough stretching along the western bank of the Hudson River in Bergen County. Once an industrial waterfront of factories and ferry landings, Edgewater has transformed into a modern residential community of luxury condominiums, townhouses, and retail centers, all set against dramatic Palisades cliffs and panoramic Manhattan skyline views.
highlights: Stunning Hudson River waterfront with unobstructed Manhattan skyline views
Major retail destination with Mitsuwa Marketplace and The Shops at Edgewater
NY Waterway ferry service to Midtown Manhattan
Gateway to the Palisades Interstate Park and scenic River Road
Rapid transformation from industrial waterfront to upscale residential community

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Edgewater's waterfront condominium developments are assessed based on unit size and river views.
realEstate.recentTrends: Edgewater's real estate market is dominated by condominiums and townhouses along the waterfront. Prices have risen steadily due to NYC proximity and ferry access. New luxury developments continue to add housing stock along River Road.
neighborhoodNotes: The waterfront corridor along River Road features luxury condo complexes like Independence Harbor, Venetian, and City Place
The Edgewater Colony neighborhood on the Palisades bluff offers older single-family homes with sweeping views
The retail corridor near Mitsuwa and Whole Foods draws significant foot traffic and anchors the commercial district
North Edgewater near the Palisades Park border has a mix of older homes and newer townhouse developments

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided twice weekly by the Borough's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days vary by location — check the borough website for your schedule
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use sealed bags or covered containers not exceeding 50 lbs
Bulk item pickup available by appointment through the DPW

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream recycling is collected weekly through the Bergen County Utilities Authority program.
details: Commingled recyclables (plastic #1-#7, glass, cans) collected in blue bins
Paper and cardboard collected separately and must be bundled or bagged
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or food-soiled items
Condo complexes may have private recycling arrangements

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Veolia Water (formerly Suez) and sewer services are managed through the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Water bills are issued quarterly by Veolia
Sewer charges appear on property tax bills
Water quality reports published annually by Veolia
Emergency water main breaks: call the DPW immediately

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Edgewater Building Department handles all construction, renovation, and zoning permit applications.
details: Permits required for any structural work, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC modifications
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board or Zoning Board of Adjustment
Certificate of Occupancy required for all new construction and changes of use
Applications submitted at Borough Hall on River Road

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Edgewater manages street parking along River Road and in municipal lots. Many condo complexes have private parking.
details: Street parking along River Road is limited and often metered
Municipal parking available near the retail areas
Overnight parking restrictions in effect on certain streets
Ferry terminal parking available for commuters

additionalServices.title: Ferry Service
additionalServices.description: NY Waterway operates ferry service from Edgewater to Midtown Manhattan.
details: Ferry terminal located at the Edgewater waterfront
Service runs to West 39th Street in Manhattan
Free shuttle bus connections to ferry from various Edgewater locations
Monthly and multi-ride passes available

additionalServices.title: Recreation Programs
additionalServices.description: The Recreation Department offers youth and adult programs, seasonal events, and community activities.
details: Youth sports programs including soccer, basketball, and baseball
Summer recreation programs for children
Community events throughout the year
Veterans Memorial Park activities and field reservations

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Edgewater enforces noise restrictions given the high density of residential condominiums and apartments.
keyRules: Construction work prohibited before 7:30 AM and after 6:00 PM on weekdays
No construction on Sundays or federal holidays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
Excessive noise from residential units subject to fines
Commercial deliveries restricted during late night hours

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners and condo associations must maintain their properties to borough standards.
keyRules: Lawns and common areas must be kept trimmed and maintained
Snow and ice must be cleared from sidewalks within 12 hours of storm ending
No accumulation of trash, debris, or abandoned items on properties
Building facades must be maintained in safe condition
Vacant properties must be registered and maintained

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dogs must be licensed annually and kept under control at all times.
keyRules: All dogs must be licensed with the Borough Clerk's office
Dogs must be on a leash when off the owner's property
Owners must clean up after their pets on public and private property
Rabies vaccination required for all dogs and cats
Condo complexes may have additional pet restrictions

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Edgewater has parking regulations addressing its dense waterfront development and River Road traffic.
keyRules: No parking on River Road during rush hours in designated areas
Overnight street parking prohibited without permit in certain zones
Fire hydrant clearance of 10 feet required at all times
No double parking — strictly enforced along River Road
Handicapped space violations carry significant fines

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Edgewater has a large rental population in its waterfront condominium and apartment complexes.
keyRules: Landlords must register rental units with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required for all rental units
Security deposit limited to 1.5 months' rent per NJ state law
Tenants protected under NJ Anti-Eviction Act
Some condo associations restrict or limit subletting
rentals.source: Edgewater Borough Code, NJ Landlord-Tenant Law

other.title: Waterfront Access
other.summary: Public access to the Hudson River waterfront is protected by state and borough regulations.
keyRules: Public walkways along the waterfront must remain accessible
No private obstruction of designated public waterfront areas
Fishing and boating subject to NJ DEP regulations
No swimming in the Hudson River from borough facilities

publicSchools.name: Eleanor Van Gelder School
publicSchools.notes: The sole public school in Edgewater; students attend Leonia High School for grades 7-12 through a send-receive relationship

privateSchools.name: Academy of Gifted Children
privateSchools.notes: Small private school emphasizing individualized instruction

nearbyHigherEd: Columbia University — NYC (via ferry or GWB, 5 miles)

whyMoveHere: Breathtaking Hudson River and Manhattan skyline views from waterfront residences
NY Waterway ferry service offers a scenic 10-minute commute to Midtown Manhattan
Modern luxury condominiums and townhouses with resort-style amenities
Mitsuwa Marketplace and diverse international dining create a unique food destination
Proximity to Palisades Interstate Park for hiking and outdoor recreation

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NY Waterway Ferry / NJ Transit Bus
commuteToNYC.duration: 10-15 minutes by ferry; 25-40 minutes by bus
commuteToNYC.notes: NY Waterway ferry runs from Edgewater to West 39th Street in Manhattan with free shuttle connections. NJ Transit buses also serve the route to Port Authority Bus Terminal. Car commute via the George Washington Bridge takes 20-45 minutes depending on traffic.

costOfLiving.summary: Edgewater's cost of living is above the national average, driven by waterfront condo prices and proximity to Manhattan, but offers value compared to living in NYC.

pros: Stunning waterfront views and modern housing stock
Fast ferry commute to Midtown Manhattan
Excellent shopping and dining including Mitsuwa Marketplace and Whole Foods
Lower property taxes compared to many Bergen County towns
Access to Palisades Interstate Park for recreation
cons: River Road traffic congestion can be severe during rush hours
Limited single-family housing options — mostly condos and townhouses
No local middle or high school — students sent to Leonia
Narrow geography limits green space and parks within the borough
Flooding risk in low-lying waterfront areas

restaurants.name: Mitsuwa Marketplace Food Court
restaurants.description: Bustling Japanese food court inside the acclaimed Mitsuwa Marketplace, featuring ramen, sushi, tempura, takoyaki, and Japanese bakery stalls.

restaurants.name: Fleming's Prime Steakhouse
restaurants.description: Upscale steakhouse chain offering prime cuts, an extensive wine list, and a polished dining experience with river views.

restaurants.name: Binghamton's
restaurants.description: Casual American restaurant and bar popular for burgers, wings, and game-day gatherings in a lively atmosphere.

restaurants.name: The Edgewater
restaurants.description: Waterfront Italian restaurant offering pasta, seafood, and brick-oven pizza with panoramic views of the Manhattan skyline.

restaurants.name: Starbucks Reserve
restaurants.description: Specialty Starbucks location at City Place offering premium coffee experiences and riverside views.

parks.name: Veterans Memorial Park
parks.description: Edgewater's main community park featuring sports fields, a playground, basketball courts, and a memorial to local veterans.

parks.name: Edgewater Waterfront Walkway
parks.description: A scenic public walkway stretching along the Hudson River waterfront, offering stunning views of the Manhattan skyline and opportunities for walking and jogging.

parks.name: Palisades Interstate Park — Edgewater Section
parks.description: The dramatic Palisades cliffs rise above Edgewater, with hiking trails accessible from the borough leading to stunning overlooks above the Hudson River.

events.name: Edgewater Street Fair
events.description: Annual community street fair along River Road featuring local vendors, food trucks, live entertainment, and family activities.

events.name: Independence Day Fireworks
events.description: July 4th fireworks display visible from the Edgewater waterfront, complementing the Macy's fireworks across the river in Manhattan.

events.name: Holiday Lighting Celebration
events.description: Annual holiday tree lighting at Veterans Memorial Park with caroling, hot chocolate, and visits from Santa.

attractions.name: Mitsuwa Marketplace
attractions.description: The largest Japanese supermarket on the East Coast, featuring a bustling food court, grocery store, bakery, bookstore, and specialty shops. A cultural landmark drawing visitors from across the tri-state area.

attractions.name: The Shops at Edgewater
attractions.description: Open-air retail center along River Road featuring national retailers, restaurants, and entertainment options with views of the Manhattan skyline.

attractions.name: Hudson River Waterfront
attractions.description: Edgewater's scenic waterfront offers unobstructed views of the Manhattan skyline, with public walkways, marinas, and ferry access to Midtown.

shoppingAreas: River Road Corridor — Major retail strip with Mitsuwa, Whole Foods, Trader Joe's, and national chains
The Promenade at Edgewater — Upscale retail and dining complex
City Place — Mixed-use development with shops and restaurants along the waterfront

government.meetingSchedule: Board of Commissioners meets on the second and fourth Wednesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Edgewater Borough Hall, 916 River Road, Edgewater, NJ 07020

hospitals.name: Hackensack Meridian Health Palisades Medical Center
